evidence.claim: [CDK9] PRIORITY 1 - strongest mechanistic rescue case. CDK9 is the P-TEFb kinase driving RNA Pol II pause-release; CDK9 physically associates with mTOR-pathway components (Beauchamp et al. 2019, Blood).
[CDK9] Mechanistic cross-talk supporting rescue: mTOR controls P-TEFb activity (mTOR phosphorylates CDK9-Thr186), so the CDK9-mTOR axis is bidirectional.
